what_constrained: Proof-grade claims needed live WMS evidence, not just plausible formulas or context scenes; several indices looked reasonable but only produced moderate/weak overlays under stricter pixel QC.
what_worked: Deterministic WMS QC plus candidate/date/zoom sweeps created a clean decision boundary: promote measured strong bookmarks, demote the rest to context, and keep Limn spill regional bookmarks honest through evidence classes.
what_differently: Build proof-target QC into the add-index workflow earlier so every new renderable Atlas layer ships with measured strong bookmark evidence before it appears as live proof.

what_constrained: Proof-grade bookmark selection was constrained more by event-window imagery quality and coordinate/source precision than by whether a spill was regulator-verified.
what_worked: Running the hotspot loop before promotion separated strong visible defaults from verified-but-weak misses; documenting rejected candidates prevented source research from disappearing.
what_differently: Resolve exact Texas RRC incident IDs and coordinates first, then run small targeted loops, instead of letting broad validation records compete with exact NMOCD rows.

what_constrained: Atlas needed account/source switching without creating a new path around the Sentinel credit and zoom guards.
what_worked: Keeping source selection as a session-scoped WMS URL resolver let the UI switch accounts while reusing the existing guarded render path.
what_differently: Write the below-zoom guard assertion before verifier review whenever a control changes provider source or spend behavior.

what_constrained: Atlas evidence cleanup was constrained by source reachability as much as source relevance; several legitimate-looking agency, journal, archive, and Google Scholar links failed automated verification through 403s, timeouts, stale URLs, or expired certificates.
what_worked: Separating cited incident/domain sources from technical imagery checks and method references gave the audit a crisp target, and pruning flaky URLs after every audit run got the catalog to 44 of 44 Gold-ready rows with zero failed URL checks.
what_differently: When adding future Atlas bookmarks, source three reachable incident/domain citations immediately and avoid citation wrappers or broad search landing pages unless they are only supporting references.

what_constrained: Leaflet default layer placement shares tilePane with basemaps, making CSS mask/clip-path operations on the overlay pane clip the basemaps underneath. Integration tests were also out-of-sync with HTML markup modifications (button counts and missing legacy state variables).
what_worked: Creating an isolated custom pane (atlasWmsPane) at z-index 350 for the overlay layers; exposing legacy state properties (overlayClipPath) in the capture state API; removing invalidateSize calls to prevent replacement tile fetches.
what_differently: Always isolate layers that require CSS masking into custom Leaflet panes to prevent parent pane styling from leaking to basemaps; keep test assertions and HTML structure changes synchronized when adding new UI views (like Mirror).

what_constrained: Wide viewports cropped out elements aligned to screen edges in the exported 1200x628 PNG; visual label overlays collided with the corner control panels on-screen; window resizing broke the Swipe clip path and Mirror container invalidation; screenshot info panels looked condensed.
what_worked: Computing dynamic crop guide offsets in JavaScript and storing them as CSS custom variables to align overlay panels; shifting compare rail to bottom-center and split labels adjacent to the divider; widening info box to 520px using grid layout, branding kicker, dynamic sensor platform badge, and inline calendar/globe/code SVG icon accents.
what_differently: Ensure that crop-based screenshot captures align all visual overlays relative to the crop frame guide rather than the viewport edges, and separate labels from data values in info boxes to avoid visual crowding.
